content.explanation:
**Want — Present Tense में चाहना:**

"Want" = चाहना (present tense)

**Positive:**
Subject + **want/wants** + to + base verb
Subject + **want/wants** + noun

🇮🇳 मैं पानी पीना चाहता हूँ।
🇬🇧 I **want** to drink water.

🇮🇳 वह नई गाड़ी चाहती है।
🇬🇧 She **wants** a new car.

**Negative:**
Subject + **do/does not want** + to + base verb

**Question:**
**Do/Does** + subject + **want** + to + verb?
rules: Want + noun: "I want water" / "She wants a job"
Want + to + verb: "I want to go" / "He wants to study"
Negative: I do not want / She does not want
Question: Do you want...? / Does she want...?
"Want" stative verb hai — "I am wanting" GALAT hai!
content.memoryTrick: **WANT = चाहना** — Simple rule: "I want + TO + verb" OR "I want + noun". कभी want+ing मत करो!

examples.hindi: वह एक अच्छी नौकरी चाहती है।
हम छुट्टी पर जाना चाहते हैं।
examples.english: We want to go on vacation.

examples.hindi: क्या तुम चाय पीना चाहते हो?
examples.english: Do you want to have tea?

mistakes.why: Want + object + to + verb (not want that...)
Does के बाद base verb (want, not wants)

speakingTips: रोज़ सुबह बोलो: "Today I want to..."
Family/friends से पूछो: "Do you want to...?"
Shopping mein use karo: "I want this in size medium please"

vocabulary.example: My desire is to travel the world.
He aspires to become a doctor.

practice.english: I want to drink water.
alternatives: i want to have water.

practice.english: She wants to go home.
alternatives: she wants to go home.

practice.english: We want to stay here.
alternatives: we want to live here.

practice.english: I want a cup of tea.
alternatives: i want some tea.

practice.english: He wants to say something.
alternatives: he wants to speak.

practice.hindi: क्या तुम कुछ खाना चाहते हो?
practice.english: Do you want to eat something?
alternatives: do you want something to eat?

practice.english: I do not want to do this.
alternatives: i don't want to do this.

practice.english: They want to learn English.
alternatives: they want to study english.

practice.english: I want to become a doctor.
alternatives: i want to be a doctor.

practice.english: Does she want to come?
alternatives: does she want to visit?

practice.hindi: मैं तुम्हारी मदद करना चाहता हूँ।
practice.english: I want to help you.
alternatives: i want to assist you.

practice.hindi: वह खुद को बेहतर बनाना चाहती है।
practice.english: She wants to improve herself.
alternatives: she wants to better herself.

practice.hindi: हम सब खुश रहना चाहते हैं।
practice.english: We all want to be happy.
alternatives: everyone wants to be happy.

practice.english: I want to apologize to him.
alternatives: i want to say sorry to him.

practice.hindi: मैं यह किताब पढ़ना चाहता हूँ।
practice.english: I want to read this book.
alternatives: i want to finish this book.

practice.english: What do you want to do?
alternatives: what would you like to do?
practice.hint: What do you want to + do?

practice.english: They want to go abroad.
alternatives: they want to travel overseas.

practice.english: I want to talk to you.
alternatives: i want to speak with you.

practice.english: He wants to become a pilot.
alternatives: he wants to be a pilot.

practice.hindi: क्या तुम छुट्टी लेना चाहते हो?
practice.english: Do you want to take a day off?
alternatives: do you want a holiday?
practice.hint: Do you want to + take a day off?

practice.hindi: मैं एक नई शुरुआत करना चाहता हूँ।
practice.english: I want to make a fresh start.
alternatives: i want to start again.
practice.hint: want to + make a fresh start

practice.hindi: वह कुछ अलग करना चाहती है।
practice.english: She wants to do something different.
alternatives: she wants to try something new.

practice.english: What do you want to become?
alternatives: what do you want to be?
practice.hint: What do you want to + become?

practice.english: I want more time.
alternatives: i need more time.

practice.hindi: वे अपनी गलतियाँ सुधारना चाहते हैं।
practice.english: They want to correct their mistakes.
alternatives: they want to fix their mistakes.

practice.hindi: मैं तुम्हारे साथ चलना चाहता हूँ।
practice.english: I want to walk with you.
alternatives: i want to go with you.

practice.hindi: वह किसी से बात नहीं करना चाहता।
practice.english: He does not want to talk to anyone.
alternatives: he doesn't want to talk to anyone.

practice.hindi: तुम किसके साथ जाना चाहते हो?
practice.english: Who do you want to go with?
alternatives: who would you like to go with?
practice.hint: Who do you want to + go with?

practice.hindi: वह कुछ खास बनाना चाहती है।
practice.english: She wants to create something special.
alternatives: she wants to make something unique.

practice.hindi: मैं अपना भविष्य खुद बनाना चाहता हूँ।
practice.english: I want to build my own future.
alternatives: i want to shape my own future.
practice.hint: want to + build my own future
